strengths: 15+ years of full-stack and distributed-systems engineering across multiple languages and domains
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency microservices (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and event-driven platforms
concerns: No demonstrated executive leadership scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people
Lacks direct fintech-infrastructure experience in credit risk, working-capital, or regulated trade decisioning
Career has been individual-contributor / senior-engineer track, not CTO-level with board-facing roadmap ownership

key_factors.factor: Production multi-agent AI systems in regulated fintech
Deep distributed-systems and low-latency engineering background

response.justification: The candidate brings a strong, broad engineering background with relevant exposure to distributed systems, low-latency services, and emerging agentic/MCP tooling, which partially aligns with the technical depth required. However, the role is an executive CTO position demanding demonstrated experience scaling 10-to-30+ engineering organizations, governing hybrid delivery models, and operating production multi-agent AI in regulated fintech — none of which are evidenced in the resume. The profile reads as a strong senior/staff engineer rather than a venture-backed scale-up CTO.
